questions.text: How does the Surah describe the 'Earthquake of the Hour' in terms of its effect on people?
questions.answer: As a terrible thing that will make every nursing mother forget her child and every pregnant woman miscarry; people will appear drunk but they are not.

questions.text: Who are those who argue about Allah without knowledge and follow every rebellious devil?
questions.answer: Those who are destined to be led astray by the devil and guided to the punishment of the Blaze.

questions.text: What are the stages of human creation from the earth mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: Dust, then a sperm-drop (Nutfah), then a clinging clot (Alaqah), then a lump of flesh (Mudghah), partly formed and partly unformed.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the life cycle of the earth when rain falls on a dead land?
questions.answer: The earth quivers, swells, and grows every kind of beautiful growth.

questions.text: What are the five certainties mentioned after the description of creation and the earth?
questions.answer: That Allah is the Truth, He gives life to the dead, He is over all things competent, the Hour is coming, and He will resurrect those in the graves.

questions.text: Describe the person who worships Allah 'on the edge'.
questions.answer: If good befalls him, he is content; but if a trial strikes him, he turns back on his face, losing both this world and the Hereafter.

questions.text: Can those whom people call upon besides Allah bring any benefit or harm?
questions.answer: No, they call upon that which neither harms nor benefits them; that is extreme error.

questions.text: Does everything in the heavens and the earth prostrate to Allah?
questions.answer: Yes, including the sun, moon, stars, mountains, trees, animals, and many of mankind.

questions.text: What will be the clothing of the disbelievers in the Fire?
questions.answer: Garments of fire will be cut out for them, and boiling water will be poured over their heads.

questions.text: What will happen to the skins and internal organs of those in Hell when boiling water is poured?
questions.answer: They will be melted by it.

questions.text: What are 'maces of iron' used for in the description of Hell?
questions.answer: For punishing the inhabitants whenever they try to escape the anguish.

questions.text: What ornaments and clothing are promised to the believers in Paradise?
questions.answer: Bracelets of gold and pearls, and their clothing will be of silk.

questions.text: What is the 'good speech' and the 'path of the Praiseworthy' the believers were guided to?
questions.answer: The declaration of faith (Tawhid) and the path of Islam.

questions.text: Who is the Sacred Mosque (Al-Masjid al-Haram) for, according to this Surah?
questions.answer: For all people equally—both the resident and the visitor.

questions.text: What was the command given to Ibrahim (AS) regarding the site of the House (Kaaba)?
questions.answer: Not to associate anything with Allah and to purify His House for those who perform Tawaf, stand in prayer, and prostrate.

questions.text: What is the significance of the 'Adhan' for Hajj mentioned in the story of Ibrahim?
questions.answer: Allah commanded him to proclaim the Hajj so people would come on foot and on every lean camel from every distant pass.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the Hajj according to this Surah?
questions.answer: That people may witness benefits for themselves and mention the name of Allah over the sacrificial animals He has provided.

questions.text: What should the pilgrims do after performing their rites and sacrifice?
questions.answer: They should complete their prescribed duties, fulfill their vows, and perform Tawaf around the Ancient House.

questions.text: What are the 'Sha'a'ir' (Symbols) of Allah?
questions.answer: The rites and signs of Allah's religion (like the sacrifice during Hajj); honoring them is from the piety of hearts.

questions.text: What reach Allah from the animal sacrifice (Qurbani)?
questions.answer: Neither their meat nor their blood reaches Allah; what reaches Him is the 'Taqwa' (piety) from your hearts.

questions.text: What is the ruling regarding the meat of the sacrificed animals?
questions.answer: The people should eat from it and feed the miserable and the poor.

questions.text: Does Allah defend the believers?
questions.answer: Yes, 'Indeed, Allah defends those who have believed. Indeed, Allah does not like everyone treacherous and ungrateful.'

questions.text: When was the first permission to fight given to the Muslims and why?
questions.answer: It was given because they were wronged and driven from their homes only because they said, 'Our Lord is Allah.'

questions.text: What would happen if Allah did not check one set of people by means of another?
questions.answer: Monasteries, churches, synagogues, and mosques—where the name of Allah is mentioned much—would have been demolished.

questions.text: What are the qualities of those whom Allah gives authority in the land?
questions.answer: They establish prayer, give Zakat, enjoin what is right, and forbid what is wrong.

questions.text: Which previous nations were mentioned as having denied their messengers?
questions.answer: The people of Nuh, 'Ad, Thamud, Ibrahim, Lut, and Madyan, as well as the people of Musa.

questions.text: How long is a 'Day' with your Lord as mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: Like a thousand years of those which you count.

questions.text: What happens to the 'wrongdoing towns' that Allah gives respite to?
questions.answer: He seizes them eventually with a terrible punishment.

questions.text: What is the 'Blindness' described in this Surah?
questions.answer: It is not the physical eyes that are blind, but the hearts within the breasts.

questions.text: What is the reward for those who emigrated for the cause of Allah and were then killed or died?
questions.answer: Allah will surely provide for them a good provision, and He is the best of providers.

questions.text: How does Allah describe His power over the night and day?
questions.answer: He causes the night to enter the day and the day to enter the night, and He is Hearing and Seeing.

questions.text: What is the proof of Allah's power regarding the sky and the earth?
questions.answer: He holds the sky from falling upon the earth except by His permission.

questions.text: What is the 'Mansak' mentioned that Allah has appointed for every nation?
questions.answer: A rite of sacrifice or worship so they may mention the name of Allah over what He provided them.

questions.text: What does Allah do with the suggestions Shaitan throws into the recitation of Prophets?
questions.answer: Allah eliminates what Shaitan throws in and then makes His verses precise.

questions.text: Who are the ones whose hearts become hardened by Shaitan's suggestions?
questions.answer: Those who have a disease in their hearts and whose hearts are hard.

questions.text: What is the reaction of the disbelievers when the clear verses of Allah are recited to them?
questions.answer: You can recognize defiance in their faces; they almost attack those who recite the verses.

questions.text: What is the parable of the 'Fly' mentioned at the end of the Surah?
questions.answer: If all those called upon besides Allah gathered together, they could not create even a fly; and if a fly took something from them, they could not recover it.

questions.text: What is the conclusion about the power of the idols and their worshippers from the fly parable?
questions.answer: 'Weak are the seeker and the sought.'

questions.text: Does Allah choose messengers from both angels and mankind?
questions.answer: Yes, 'Allah chooses from the angels messengers and from the people.'

questions.text: What are the final commands given to the believers in the last ayah?
questions.answer: Bow, prostrate, worship your Lord, and do good—that you may succeed.

questions.text: What is the 'religion of your father Ibrahim' referred to in the end?
questions.answer: Pure monotheism (Haneefiyyah) and total submission to Allah.

questions.text: What title did Allah give to the believers from before and in this Quran?
questions.answer: Muslims.

questions.text: What is the purpose of being 'witnesses over mankind' as mentioned in the conclusion?
questions.answer: The Messenger is a witness over the Muslims, and the Muslims are witnesses over mankind.

questions.text: What is the final advice regarding holding fast to Allah?
questions.answer: Hold fast to Allah; He is your protector—and an excellent protector and excellent helper.

questions.text: What is the meaning of the name 'Al-Hajj'?
questions.answer: The Pilgrimage.

questions.text: What is the 'Ancient House' (Al-Bayt al-Atiq)?
questions.answer: The Kaaba in Makkah.

questions.text: What is the 'abject' state of man mentioned in the creation passage?
questions.answer: Old age, where a person knows nothing after having had knowledge.

questions.text: What is the 'Disease of the heart'?
questions.answer: Doubt, hypocrisy, and lack of sincere faith.

questions.text: What is the 'True Sovereign' (Al-Malik al-Haqq)?
questions.answer: An attribute of Allah, meaning the only real and permanent King.

questions.text: What are the 'signs' in the falling of rain on dead land?
questions.answer: Proofs of Allah's ability to resurrect the dead.

questions.text: Why does Allah mention 'monasteries and churches'?
questions.answer: To show that His protection of religious freedom through struggle preserves places where He is worshipped.

questions.text: What is the 'Great Bounty' (Al-Fadl al-Kabir)?
questions.answer: The rewards of Paradise for the believers.

questions.text: What is the 'Humble' (Al-Mukhbitin) heart?
questions.answer: A heart that is submissive, fearful, and tranquil in the remembrance of Allah.

questions.text: What are the 'Camels' (Budn) in the context of Hajj?
questions.answer: Large animals made as symbols of Allah's bounty for sacrifice.

questions.text: What is the 'Right Path' (Siratin Mustaqim)?
questions.answer: The way of life that leads to Allah's pleasure and success.

questions.text: What is the 'Anguish' (Ghamm) of Hell?
questions.answer: The intense physical and mental suffering of the disbelievers.

questions.text: What is the 'Truth' (Al-Haqq) that Allah is?
questions.answer: The ultimate Reality and the only one worthy of worship.

questions.text: What is the 'Specified Term' (Ajal Musamma)?
questions.answer: The fixed time for everything, including the Hour.

questions.text: What is the 'Submission' (Aslimu) required of people?
questions.answer: Total surrender to the will of the one God.

questions.text: What is the 'Good News' for those who are humble?
questions.answer: The promise of Paradise and Allah's approval.

questions.text: What is the 'Sacred House'?
questions.answer: The Kaaba, which is the center of Hajj.

questions.text: What is the 'Ancient' attribute of the House?
questions.answer: It signifies its long history since the time of Ibrahim and its noble status.

questions.text: What is the 'Vow' (Nudhur) in Hajj?
questions.answer: Personal commitments made to Allah during the pilgrimage.

questions.text: What is the 'Stumbling' of the person who worships on the edge?
questions.answer: His quick departure from faith when things go wrong.

questions.text: What is the 'Boiling Water' (Hamim)?
questions.answer: A punishment in Hell that melts what is inside and outside.

questions.text: What is the 'Broccoli' (Mudghah) stage?
questions.answer: A lump of flesh in embryonic development, literally meaning 'something chewed'.

questions.text: What is the 'Clot' (Alaqah) stage?
questions.answer: A stage in human development where the embryo clings to the womb.

questions.text: What is the 'Sperm-drop' (Nutfah) stage?
questions.answer: The initial stage of biological human life.

questions.text: What is the 'Dust' (Turab) origin?
questions.answer: The chemical components of the earth from which Adam was created.

questions.text: What is the 'Distraction' of the Hour?
questions.answer: The sheer terror that will make people forget their most precious bonds.

questions.text: What is the 'Drunkenness' without alcohol?
questions.answer: The state of confusion and shock people will be in on the Day of Judgment.

questions.text: What is the 'rebellious devil'?
questions.answer: Any force or being that leads people away from the truth.

questions.text: What is the 'Balance' (Qawwam)?
questions.answer: The perfect ecological harmony in nature created by Allah.

questions.text: What is the 'Certainty' regarding the Hour?
questions.answer: That its arrival is a factual reality beyond any doubt.

questions.text: What is the 'Resurrection' (Ba'th)?
questions.answer: The bringing of the dead back to life for judgment.

questions.text: What is the 'Edge' (Harf)?
questions.answer: A weak and unstable position in faith.

questions.text: What is the 'Extreme Error' (Dalal al-Ba'id)?
questions.answer: Worshipping idols instead of the Creator.

questions.text: What is the 'Great Anguish'?
questions.answer: The suffering of the people in Hell.

questions.text: What is the ' ancient House' Tawaf?
questions.answer: The ritual of walking around the Kaaba during Hajj and Umrah.

questions.text: What is the 'Success' (Falah)?
questions.answer: The ultimate achievement of Paradise and Allah's pleasure.
